assessor_raw: The principal problem with this manuscript is that the outcome variable changes meaning during the study period. The ICU transfer endpoint is explicitly described as dependent on bed availability, triage policy, and physician escalation thresholds — and all three of these changed at one of the two sites during the evaluation window. The rapid-response reorganization removes complex respiratory cases from the ward population, mechanically depressing the positive label rate at that site. The tele-intensivist overnight program shifts borderline transfer decisions to a remote reviewer. Both changes happen independent of patient clinical acuity. A model trained and evaluated across this boundary is learning a mixed signal: clinical deterioration plus institutional logistics. Pooling this site with the other (stable) site on the basis of shared EHR build is not a valid equalization of confounders. The absence of site-stratified results, calibration analysis, or any physiologic endpoint comparison makes it impossible to assess whether the prospective improvement is genuine. The clinical deployment recommendation is not justified by the evidence.
